clause_text: 1. Demise and Description Landlord hereby leases to Tenant, and Tenant accepts, the commercial unit described in Exhibit A together with non-exclusive rights over common accessways. The leasehold estate includes only the areas expressly identified and excludes the roof, structure and service rooms unless otherwise stated.

2. Initial Term The initial term is five years beginning on the commencement date recorded in the delivery certificate. Tenant may exercise one renewal option for an additional three-year term by giving not less than nine months' prior written notice and provided no uncured event of default exists.

3. Base Rent Tenant shall pay annual base rent in equal monthly installments in advance without deduction, setoff or demand. Base rent increases on each anniversary by the percentage or fixed amount stated in the rent schedule.

4. Additional Rent Tenant shall pay as additional rent its proportionate share of operating expenses, common-area maintenance charges, real estate taxes and insurance costs allocated to the building. Landlord shall provide an annual reconciliation with reasonable supporting detail.

5. Permitted Use Tenant may use the premises solely for the operation of a professional services office and for no other purpose without Landlord's prior written approval. Tenant shall obtain all licenses and shall comply with laws applicable to its specific business operations.

6. Delivery and Condition Landlord shall deliver possession in broom-clean condition with base building systems serving the premises operational. Except for express Landlord work, Tenant accepts the premises in their existing condition after completing its own inspection.

7. Tenant Improvements Tenant may construct approved improvements only in accordance with plans reviewed by Landlord and all governmental requirements. Alterations affecting structure, exterior appearance, building systems or life-safety equipment require specific written consent and qualified contractors.

8. Repairs Tenant shall maintain the interior, non-structural components, fixtures and equipment exclusively serving the premises. Landlord shall maintain the foundation, roof, exterior walls and common building systems, except for damage caused by Tenant or its contractors.

9. Compliance with Laws Each party shall comply with laws applicable to its obligations. Tenant is responsible for occupancy permits and business-specific accessibility requirements triggered by Tenant's use or alterations, while Landlord remains responsible for base-building compliance not caused by Tenant.

10. Insurance Tenant shall maintain commercial general liability, property, workers' compensation and business interruption insurance in commercially reasonable amounts. Landlord and its property manager shall be named as additional insureds where customary, and certificates shall be delivered before occupancy.

11. Indemnity Tenant shall indemnify and hold harmless Landlord from third-party claims arising from Tenant's negligence, willful misconduct, business operations or breach, except to the extent caused by Landlord's negligence or willful misconduct. Landlord provides a reciprocal indemnity for claims arising from its own misconduct.

12. Assignment Tenant shall not assign the lease, sublet all or any portion, license occupancy, or transfer a controlling ownership interest without Landlord's prior written consent. A permitted transfer does not release Tenant unless Landlord expressly agrees in writing.

13. Utilities Tenant shall contract and pay for separately metered utilities and telecommunications. Shared utility costs may be allocated based on submetering, usage estimates or rentable area, provided the allocation method is applied consistently.

14. Damage and Casualty If a casualty materially impairs use of the premises, rent shall abate proportionately during restoration. Either party may terminate if restoration cannot reasonably be completed within the outside restoration period stated in a written architect's estimate.

15. Eminent Domain If all or a material part of the premises is taken by governmental authority, either party may terminate. Awards for Landlord's property interest belong to Landlord, while Tenant may pursue a separate claim for relocation costs and trade fixtures where legally permitted.

16. Events of Default Events of default include failure to pay rent after required notice, failure to perform a material covenant after any applicable cure period, abandonment, insolvency events to the extent enforceable, or an unauthorized transfer.

17. Remedies Following an uncured default, Landlord may terminate possession, recover sums due, re-enter as permitted by law, and pursue damages subject to the duty to mitigate where applicable. Remedies are cumulative but no remedy permits an unlawful forfeiture.

18. Surrender and Holdover At expiration, Tenant shall surrender vacant possession, remove movable trade fixtures, repair removal damage and return access credentials. A holdover accepted by Landlord creates only a month-to-month tenancy at the stated holdover rent unless the parties sign a renewal.

19. Notices Formal notices shall be delivered to the addresses stated in the lease by personal delivery, nationally recognized overnight courier, or certified mail. Operational communications by email do not constitute formal notice unless expressly acknowledged.

20. Dispute Resolution Before filing suit, senior representatives shall meet in good faith to attempt resolution. The parties may agree to mediation, but either party may seek emergency injunctive relief or pursue claims in a court having jurisdiction over the premises.

21. Environmental Matters Tenant shall not generate, release or store hazardous substances except ordinary office products used in lawful quantities. Tenant shall promptly report any spill and remediate contamination caused by its operations.

22. Confidentiality Non-public financial statements, operating data and proprietary information exchanged in connection with this lease shall be kept confidential, except for disclosure to lenders, advisers, insurers or governmental authorities having a legitimate need to know.

EXECUTION This agreement is executed by duly authorized representatives of Landlord and Tenant. Electronic signatures and counterparts shall be valid, and each signatory represents that he or she has authority to bind the party identified below. LANDLORD / LESSOR: ________________________________ DATE: _______________ TENANT / LESSEE: __________________________________ DATE: _______________ WITNESS (if required): _____________________________ DATE: _______________

1. Recitals WHEREAS the Lessor is seized and possessed of the land and hereditaments described in the Schedule, and WHEREAS the Lessor has agreed to grant a lease thereof to the Lessee upon the covenants and conditions hereinafter appearing.

2. Operative Grant NOW THIS DEED WITNESSETH that in consideration of the rent reserved and the covenants of the Lessee, the Lessor hereby demises unto the Lessee ALL THAT parcel of land described in the Schedule TO HOLD for the term stated below.

3. Habendum and Term The demised premises shall be held for a term of twenty-one years commencing on the first day of [month and year], subject nevertheless to earlier determination in accordance with the proviso for re-entry and the other provisions of this deed.

4. Reserved Rent The Lessee shall yearly and every year pay the rent reserved by equal quarterly payments in advance without any deduction, the first payment being apportioned from the commencement date.

5. Rates and Outgoings The Lessee shall bear and discharge all rates, assessments, duties, charges for water and utilities, and other outgoings imposed upon the occupier, except taxes assessed solely upon the Lessor's ownership interest.

6. Repair Covenant The Lessee shall at all times keep the demised premises, walls, roofs, drains, fixtures and appurtenances in good and substantial repair and condition, fair wear and damage by insured risks excepted where the insurance proceeds are made available for reinstatement.
7. Decoration The Lessee shall paint and decorate the interior at reasonable intervals and in the final year of the term, using materials and workmanship of suitable quality.

8. Alterations The Lessee shall not pull down, alter, add to or erect any building or structure upon the demised premises without the previous consent in writing of the Lessor and all approvals required by law.
9. User Covenant The Lessee shall use the demised premises only for the permitted trade stated in the Schedule and shall not carry on any noisy, noxious, dangerous or unlawful occupation.

10. Alienation The Lessee shall not assign, underlet, charge or part with possession of the whole or any part of the demised premises without the previous written consent of the Lessor, such consent being subject to lawful conditions.

11. Insurance The Lessor shall insure the buildings against the usual insured risks for their full reinstatement value, and the Lessee shall reimburse the premium as additional rent within fourteen days after written demand supported by evidence of payment.

12. Indemnity The Lessee shall indemnify the Lessor against liabilities, proceedings, costs and claims occasioned by the negligent act, omission or default of the Lessee, its servants, agents, licensees or invitees.

13. Inspection The Lessee shall permit the Lessor and its surveyors upon reasonable notice to enter and inspect the state of repair and to serve notice specifying defects for which the Lessee is responsible.

14. Compliance Notice Within a reasonable time after service of a repair notice, the Lessee shall commence and diligently complete the required works, failing which the Lessor may enter, carry out the works and recover the reasonable cost as a debt.

15. Quiet Enjoyment The Lessee paying the rent and observing the covenants may peaceably and quietly hold and enjoy the demised premises during the term without any lawful interruption by the Lessor or any person claiming under the Lessor.

16. Proviso for Re-entry If rent is in arrears for twenty-one days after becoming due, whether formally demanded or not, or if the Lessee materially breaches a covenant and fails to remedy after required notice, the Lessor may re-enter as permitted by law and the term shall determine.

17. Damage by Insured Risk Where the premises are rendered unfit by an insured risk, the rent or a fair proportion thereof shall be suspended until reinstatement, unless insurance recovery is prejudiced by the Lessee's act or default.

18. Yielding Up At the expiration or earlier determination of the term, the Lessee shall yield up the premises with vacant possession in the repair and condition required by this deed and shall remove signs and movable fixtures if directed.
19. Notices A notice required by this deed shall be in writing and may be served personally, by registered post, or by another method recognized by the law governing the premises.
20. Schedule The Schedule shall identify the premises, plan reference, commencement date, annual rent, permitted use, insurance contribution, and any special rights of way or reservations.

EXECUTION IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed and delivered this deed on the date first written above. The execution of this instrument may occur in counterparts, each counterpart being treated as an original. Signed, sealed and delivered by the Lessor in the presence of a witness, and signed, sealed and delivered by the Lessee in the presence of a witness. LANDLORD / LESSOR: ________________________________ DATE: _______________ TENANT / LESSEE: __________________________________ DATE: _______________ WITNESS (if required): _____________________________ DATE: _______________

1. Creation of Tenancy Landlord rents the dwelling to Tenant on a month-to-month basis beginning on [date]. The tenancy continues for successive monthly periods until terminated by either party through written notice that complies with the minimum period required by local law.

2. Monthly Rent Rent of [amount] is due on the first calendar day of each month. If the tenancy begins on another date, the first payment shall be prorated, and later payments remain due on the first unless the parties sign a different written schedule.
3. Deposit Tenant shall provide a refundable deposit of [amount]. No part of the deposit may be treated as the last month's rent without Landlord's written consent.
4. Rent Changes Landlord may change rent only through advance written notice and only to the extent permitted by applicable rent-control, consumer-protection or tenancy law.

5. Occupancy The dwelling may be occupied by Tenant and the listed household members. Tenant shall notify Landlord before adding a long-term occupant and shall not create overcrowded or unsafe conditions.

6. Residential Use The property is for ordinary residential use. Home-office activity is allowed only if it is lawful, does not create customer traffic or nuisance, and does not materially increase utility consumption or insurance risk.

7. Care of Property Tenant shall keep the premises sanitary, dispose of waste properly and notify Landlord promptly of conditions requiring repair. Tenant shall be responsible for damage caused by misuse or neglect.

8. Landlord Maintenance Landlord shall maintain building systems, structural components and legally required habitability conditions, subject to reasonable access and any lawful allocation of minor maintenance duties.
9. Locks and Security Tenant shall not replace or add locks without permission except where emergency or safety law permits. Tenant shall provide Landlord with any lawfully required access key.
10. Pets No pet may be kept without written approval, except an assistance animal protected by law. Any approved pet must be controlled and may not damage property or disturb neighbors.
11. Smoking Smoking and vaping are prohibited inside the dwelling and in any designated smoke-free common area. This restriction does not guarantee a smoke-free environment.
12. Parking Any assigned parking space is personal to Tenant and may not be transferred. Vehicles must be registered, operable and parked in compliance with posted rules.
13. Entry and Inspection Landlord may enter on lawful notice for repairs, inspections, pest treatment, appraisal or showing, and may enter without prior notice in a genuine emergency.
14. Subletting Tenant shall not sublet, assign, host paying guests, or list the premises on a short-term rental platform without prior written approval.
15. Termination by Tenant Tenant may end the tenancy by giving written notice at least as long as required by governing law. Rent remains payable through the lawful termination date.
16. Termination by Landlord Landlord may terminate only for a reason and through a procedure permitted by law, including any required cause, notice, cure opportunity and court process.

17. Abandonment If Tenant appears to have abandoned the premises, Landlord may secure and recover possession only under the applicable abandonment rules and may not unlawfully dispose of personal property.
18. Quiet Possession Tenant is entitled to quiet possession without unreasonable disturbance, subject to Landlord's lawful rights of access and the rights of other residents.
19. Notices Each party shall keep a current mailing address and email address for routine communication. Statutory notices must be served by a legally authorized method.

EXECUTION The parties sign below to acknowledge that they have read and accepted this agreement. Signatures may be handwritten or electronic, and counterparts together constitute one instrument. LANDLORD / LESSOR: ________________________________ DATE: _______________ TENANT / LESSEE: __________________________________ DATE: _______________ WITNESS (if required): _____________________________ DATE: _______________

1. Parties and Premises The Landlord lets to the Tenant the dwelling described as [property address], together with the ordinary right to use the entrances, corridors, shared grounds and appurtenances serving the dwelling. The premises are accepted for residential occupation only and no ownership interest is transferred.

2. Term and Renewal The tenancy begins on [start date] and continues for twelve months unless earlier terminated in accordance with this agreement. Any renewal must be confirmed in writing, and continued occupation after expiry does not by itself create a new fixed term unless applicable law provides otherwise.

3. Rent The Tenant shall pay monthly rent of [amount] in advance on the first day of each month by the payment method designated by the Landlord. Rent is deemed paid when cleared funds are received, and partial payment does not waive any remaining balance.

4. Security Deposit The Tenant shall pay a security deposit of [amount]. The deposit may be applied to unpaid rent, damage beyond ordinary wear, cleaning required because of the Tenant's breach, or other lawful charges, subject to an itemized accounting and applicable deposit law.

5. Late Payment If rent remains unpaid after any legally required grace period, the Landlord may impose a reasonable late charge stated in a written notice. Acceptance of a late charge does not prevent the Landlord from pursuing other lawful remedies for continuing default.

6. Use of Premises The premises shall be used only as a private residence by the Tenant and approved occupants. The Tenant shall not conduct unlawful activity, create a nuisance, store hazardous materials, or use the property in a manner that materially increases insurance risk.

7. Occupants and Guests Only the persons identified in the tenancy record may reside at the premises. Guests may stay temporarily, but repeated or extended occupancy by a guest requires the Landlord's prior written consent where permitted by law.

8. Repairs and Maintenance The Tenant shall keep the interior reasonably clean, promptly report leaks or unsafe conditions, and use fixtures in a proper manner. The Landlord shall perform structural and other repairs allocated to the Landlord by law, except to the extent damage was caused by the Tenant or the Tenant's guests.

9. Alterations The Tenant shall not paint, install fixtures, change locks, make structural alterations, or attach exterior equipment without the Landlord's prior written consent. Approved improvements become part of the premises unless the Landlord requires restoration at the end of the tenancy.

10. Utilities and Taxes The Tenant shall pay the utilities specifically allocated to the Tenant, including metered electricity, gas, internet and similar services. The Landlord shall remain responsible for real property taxes and any utility expressly identified as included in rent.

11. Insurance The Tenant is encouraged to maintain renter's insurance covering personal property and personal liability. The Landlord's insurance does not cover the Tenant's belongings, temporary accommodation, or losses for which the Tenant is responsible.

12. Entry Upon reasonable notice, the Landlord may enter the premises to inspect, perform repairs, supply agreed services, show the property to prospective tenants or purchasers, or address a suspected emergency. No notice is required where immediate entry is reasonably necessary to protect persons or property.

13. Assignment and Subletting The Tenant shall not assign this agreement, sublet any part of the premises, advertise the premises for short-term occupancy, or otherwise transfer possession without the Landlord's prior written consent, which may be withheld to the extent permitted by law.

14. Quiet Enjoyment So long as the Tenant pays rent and observes the material terms of this agreement, the Tenant may peaceably and quietly possess and enjoy the premises without unreasonable interference by the Landlord or persons claiming through the Landlord.

15. Default and Termination A material failure to pay rent, serious damage, unlawful use, unauthorized transfer, or other substantial breach constitutes a default. The non-defaulting party may deliver any notice required by law and may terminate only after expiration of the applicable cure or notice period.

16. Surrender At the end of the tenancy, the Tenant shall vacate, remove personal property, return all keys and access devices, and leave the premises reasonably clean, ordinary wear excepted. Property left behind may be handled in accordance with applicable abandoned-property law.

17. Notices Notices under this agreement shall be in writing and delivered personally, by recognized courier, by certified mail, or by another method permitted by law. A notice is effective when delivered or deemed delivered under the governing legal rules.

18. Governing Law This agreement is governed by the law of the jurisdiction in which the premises are located. Any unenforceable provision shall be limited or severed without invalidating the remaining provisions.

THE LONDON BOROUGH OF EALING 1. SECURE TENANCY AGREEMENT This tenancy agreement explains the terms and conditions you must keep to and what you can expect from the landlord. If you do not understand anything in this agreement you should ask about it before you sign. In signing this agreement you are agreeing to keep to the terms and conditions and if you do not the landlord can take legal action to force you to do so and /or to repossess the property.
1.1 This agreement is made between: THE LANDLORD The landlord is the London Borough of Ealing THE TENANT
(S) The persons listed in the box below are the tenants and where a joint tenancy exists they are jointly and severally liable for all the terms of the tenancy. Surname First name DOB NI number
1.2 Tenancy commencement date This tenancy is a weekly periodic tenancy commencing on: …………………………..

1.3 The landlord’s main address The London Borough of Ealing Perceval House 14-16 Uxbridge Road London W5 2HL This is the landlord’s address for the serving of notices, including notices in court proceedings

1.4 False information If it is later discovered that false information has been given to gain the tenancy then action will be taken to repossess the property under schedule 2 ground 5 of the Housing Act 1985.

1.5 Data protection Act Personal information supplied by a tenant to the Council for the purposes of entering into this agreement will not be disclosed to other persons except in accordance with the requirements of the Data Protection Act 1998. Examples of reasons why personal information may be disclosed are for the purposes of preventing or detecting crime, apprehending or prosecuting offenders or assessing or collecting tax.

1.6 Rights of third parties The provision of the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999 will not apply to this tenancy agreement, which means that not one of the terms of this tenancy agreement can be enforced other than by you or the landlord.

1.7 The property This is the home let to you and in this agreement “the property" means your home plus any garden, garage, shed, out building, fence and wall. The landlord grants and you accept the tenancy of this property starting on the tenancy commencement date above subject to the terms and conditions in this agreement. Property address Floor Number of bed rooms Date gas safety certificate provided Value of decoration vouchers Proof of ID and passport photo of tenant(s)
1.8 Persons residing in the property (other than the tenant(s)) Surname Other name Relationship to tenant Date of birth

1.9 The rent and service charges The tenancy is a weekly periodic tenancy commencing on any Monday. The weekly rent plus service charges is £…………… payable in advance on every Monday. Charges Amount Net Rent Water and sewage Community Alarm Supporting People services CCTV Garage Other Gross Weekly Rent THE CONDITIONS OF TENANCY
2. THE LANDLORD’S RIGHTS 2.1 Alteration to Rent and Other Charges The landlord may alter the rent and any other charges, such as Supporting People charges, by giving you four weeks notice in writing,

2.2 Variation of the Tenancy Conditions The landlord has the right to change the tenancy conditions by giving you four weeks notice and following statutory procedures in accordance with section 103 of the Housing Act 1985.

2.3 Serving of Notices The landlord has the right to serve notices of any kind in connection with this agreement on you in person, by leaving them at the property or by sending them to you at the property by ordinary pre-paid post.

2.4 Right of Entry to the Property 2.4.1 The landlord has the right of entry to the property on 24 hours' notice but will wherever possible give more then 24 hours notice. The landlord may delay entry where the tenant agrees to an appointment within a reasonable time. Whenever possible written notice will be given but this is not essential, in the following circumstances:
(a) To carry out repair or improvement or make any installations in or to the property or to any part of the landlord’s premises.
(b) To carry out disinfestations, fumigation or pest control in the property.
(c) To inspect and survey the property and to record details on the condition of the property which causes the landlord concern.
(d) To carry out servicing of any gas or electrical supply or appliances at the property.
(e) To comply with any statutory obligation of the landlord in respect of the property or any part of the landlord’s premises.
(f) To reinstate, make good, remove or repair any neglect, damage or unauthorised alteration or addition to the property.

2.4.2 The landlord has the right of entry to the property without notice in the case of an emergency .An emergency includes fire; flood; leaks; any imminent risk of physical harm to any person; imminent risk of significant damage to the property or any other property; the property is unoccupied and inadequately secured against unauthorised entry or vandalism. In an emergency the landlord has the right to enter the property without notice to do what is reasonably required to deal with the emergency in an appropriate manner and to record the condition of the property. Any damage caused when entering the property will be made good and afterwards the property will be secured against unauthorised entry.

3. THE LANDLORD’S RESPONSIBILITIES 3.1 Repairs and Maintenance In accordance with section 11 of the Landlord & Tenant Act 1985 the landlord will keep in repair the structure, exterior of the building, common parts. and all installations for the supply of water, gas, electricity, heating and sanitation, including baths, basins, toilets and sinks provided by the landlord. Common parts include corridors, walkways, stairs, lifts and gardens used by you and others. In sheltered /very sheltered schemes the landlord will keep in good repair furniture and furnishings in common parts. The landlord will carry out repairs within a reasonable time giving priority to emergency repairs.
3.2 Decorations The landlord will decorate the exterior of the property and the common parts as the landlord considers necessary.

4. THE TENANT'S RIGHTS 4.1 Right to occupy You have the right to occupy the property without interruption or interference from the landlord for the duration of this tenancy, so long as you comply with this agreement.

4.2 Security of tenure You have security of tenure so long as you, or in the case of a joint tenancy at least one of you, occupies the property as his/her only or principal home in accordance with the terms of this agreement. If a Notice to Quit is given by one of two (or more) joint tenants the tenancy will end for both (or all) when the Notice expires.

5.1.1 You or your joint tenant must occupy the property as your only or principal home and will inform the landlord in advance if not occupying for a period longer than six weeks with details of access arrangements.
5.1.2 You must inform the landlord immediately of any changes in the household and must not cause the property to become statutorily overcrowded.
5.1.3 You are responsible for your actions and for those of anyone living in or visiting the property and you must ensure that this tenancy agreement is not breached.

5.1.4 If you are a tenant living in sheltered housing you must not allow other people to live with you without the prior permission of the landlord in writing. Permission will only be given in exceptional circumstances.

5.1.5 If the agreement is breached the landlord may commence legal proceedings, which could include possession of the property, demoting the tenancy, injunctions and orders with powers of arrest, and will work with other agencies to ensure a safe neighbourhood within the scope of the law.
5.2.1 You must pay the rent and any other charges due under the terms of this agreement every week in advance.

5.2.2 If you require support services you must pay Supporting People charges under the terms of this agreement every week in advance for housing related services provided in sheltered housing and other accommodation where support is provided. These charges will be notified to you and may increase from time to time as advised by the landlord.
5.2.3 The landlord will take legal action to recover unpaid rent and charges and this may result in the court ordering you to pay court costs.

5.3.1 You must decorate the internal parts of the property and repair/replace appliances, fixtures and fittings that were not provided by the landlord. If you are elderly and/or disabled you may qualify for assistance under the landlords decorating assistance scheme.
5.3.2 You must report all repairs and defects in the property to the landlord immediately, except for those that you are responsible for as listed below:
(a) Unblock local blockages of sinks, basins, toilets and baths. (b) Repair/replace door furniture such as handles, letterboxes, cupboard catches and hinges.
(c) Repair/replace WC seats, bath panels, plugs and chains on baths, basins and sinks.
(d) Replace glass in windows, unless it is caused by vandalism or a break in and a Police Crime Number is supplied.
(e) Ease door, locks and window hinges. (f) Repair minor plaster cracks and internal decorations and maintain wall and floor tiles.
(g) Replace (unless you live in sheltered housing) keys and locks when keys are lost or members of the household are locked out.
(h) Repair electrical faults arising from the use of faulty appliances.
(i) Repair/replace electrical plugs (not socket outlets) and fuses. (j) Repair/replace internal doors and locks.
(k) Fit waste and supply pipe work for washing machines and dishwashers and vents for tumble driers.

(l) Keep gully grids clear of leaves and rubbish. Housing will undertake some of these repairs for elderly and disabled tenants who are unable to carry them out and have no one else at the property who can undertake the repairs.

5.3.3 You are responsible for any repairs that have resulted from any act by you, your household, your visitors or any 3rd party you have employed. This will include damage caused by a forced entry by the Police in accessing the property.
5.3.4 Any works undertaken by you must comply with all current building regulations and the employment of suitably qualified contractors for all gas and electrical services.
5.3.5 You must pay for any damage to the property or any other property caused by a failure of your own appliances.
5.3.6 Where the landlord has installed a smoke alarm in the property you are responsible for testing it on a regular basis and for replacing batteries.
5.4 Alterations to the Premises You must not, without first obtaining the written consent of the landlord:
(a) Make any structural alterations (including windows), or additions to the property or decorate the exterior of the property.
(b) Remove or alter the landlord’s fixed units, doors, fixtures or fittings.
(c) Erect a shed, greenhouse, conservatory or lean-to or other buildings or structure in or on the property.
(d) Plant any trees, or shrubs that may cause damage to the property or any neighbouring property.

(e) Remove or cut down any trees on the property. (f) Affix or hang any TV aerial, satellite dish or advertisement or any other thing to or from the exterior of the property. This consent may be subject to conditions that you must comply with.

5.5 Condition of the Property You must keep the property in a reasonable condition and take reasonable steps to prevent infestation and condensation. You are responsible for cleaning your own windows and extractor fans where accessible.

5.6 Rubbish and refuse You must dispose of all rubbish and refuse securely, safely and hygienically. You are responsible for arranging disposal of any material not usually collected by the refuse collector. You must not place or store any rubbish or materials against any outside walls of the property.

5.7 Common Parts and Areas You must keep the common areas outside your home and adjacent to the property clean, free from obstruction and tidy at all times and must pay for: a) The cleaning of lifts and communal areas if fouled by you, your household, visitors or pets. b) The clearing of blocked rubbish chutes blocked by inappropriate disposal of rubbish by you, your household or visitors. c) The clearing of refuse deposited in the common parts by you, your household or visitors.

5.8 Recharges You must provide access to the property on 24 hours notice, or without notice in case of emergency, to the landlord to undertake whatever is necessary to make good any damage to the property, it’s fixtures or fittings, caused by a breach of your responsibilities under this agreement. You will be recharged all reasonable costs incurred where the landlord is required to access and make good any damage to the property caused by you.

5.9 Parking and Vehicle Repairs You, your household and visitors, must comply with any parking conditions issued by the landlord and must not obstruct access to other property, the highway or emergency access areas. You, your household and visitors must not park any untaxed or un-roadworthy motor vehicle, industrial or heavy goods vehicles, caravan, boat, trailer etc on the landlord’s land without the landlords written permission. You, your household and visitors must not carry out or allow to be carried out any vehicle repairs at the property or on any Council land, including on estate roads, estate parking areas or other parts of the estate.

5.10 Gardens and Patios You must maintain your own gardens, trees, hedges, window boxes, patios, fencing and gates to a reasonable standard. You must repair/replace inter garden fencing and gates and rear garden paths.

5.11 Animals You must not keep at the property any animal or pet without the prior written permission of the landlord and must not keep the animal at the property if permission is withdrawn. If permission is given subject to conditions you must comply with the conditions. The landlord will not grant permission or will withdraw permission if the animal is or is likely to become a nuisance, annoyance or health hazard to any neighbour or person within the locality. If permission is given you will be responsible for the animal at all times and must not allow the animal into communal areas unattended or without a lead.

5.12.1 You may assign the tenancy of the property as permitted by section 91 and 92 of the Housing Act 1985.Before assigning the tenancy to another secure tenant in accordance with Section 92 you must obtain the prior permission of the landlord in writing.
5.12.2 You must not sub-let the whole of the property in any circumstances. Where this occurs the landlord will take legal action to repossess the property.
5.12.3 You may sub-let part of the property with the prior permission of the landlord in writing. The landlord will not unreasonably refuse you permission to sub-let part of the property to a lodger.

5.13 Use of the Dwelling You, your household and visitors must not use the property for any illegal or immoral purpose or to store any gas bottles or cylinders, petrol canisters, weapons or inflammable substance. You must not use the property for running a business or allow any other person to do so without the written permission of the landlord and without appropriate planning consent. You or any other person must not advertise or display notices or signs for business purposes on the property without the written permission of the landlord.

5.14.1 You are responsible for the behaviour of anyone who lives at or visits the property. The landlord will treat any breach of this agreement by others as your breach. You, your household and your visitors must not cause a nuisance or disturbance to any person or commit any acts of harassment to any person within the property, common parts or locality.
5.14.2 Harassment includes (but is not limited to) the following: (a) Harassment by reason of age, disability race, gender, religion, culture or sexuality.
(b) Violence or threats of violence to any persons (c) Abusive or insulting words or behaviour.
(d) Damage or threat of damage to property belonging to the landlord or another person.
(e) Writing any form of graffiti on Council property. (f) Making unnecessary or excessive noise.
(g) Involvement in any criminal activity. (h) Any act or omission calculated to inconvenience or interfere with the peace or comfort of any person.

5.15 Domestic violence You must not cause or tolerate other members of the household to use or threaten to use violence against anyone else living in the property. Domestic violence includes, but is not limited to, physical, sexual, emotional and financial abuse and the imposition of social isolation. If you cause or permit domestic violence the landlord may take legal action to evict you.

5.16 Drugs You, your household and visitors must not store, use or supply drugs from or within the property, common parts or any other of the landlord’s property unless they are being stored for a lawful prescribed medicinal use. If you breach this clause, the landlord will co-operate with the Police and other agencies and this may result in possession of the property and/or arrest by the Police.

5.17 Threats and Violence Against Staff You, your household and visitors must not threaten, verbally abuse or assault any member of staff employed by the landlord, its agents or contractors. If you breach this condition the landlord may restrict your access to staff, contractors or offices in such circumstances as are considered by the landlord as appropriate and may also commence legal proceedings against you.

5.18 Terminating the Tenancy You may terminate the tenancy by giving the landlord four weeks notice to quit in writing and signed by you. You must allow access for the landlord to inspect the property within the four week notice period. Your secure tenancy or a demoted tenancy may be terminated by the landlord by serving on you the appropriate statutory notice where required. The court allows the landlord not to serve such a notice in certain cases of anti-social behaviour when obtaining a court order for possession. If your tenancy ceases to be a secure tenancy or a demoted tenancy the landlord may terminate the tenancy by giving you notice to quit.

5.19.1 You must pay all outstanding debts due under the terms of the tenancy, leave and give up vacant possession of the property. You must remove all your belongings and anything that belongs to another person and leave the property clear of refuse. The property should be empty, including the removal of carpets, except for fixtures and fittings that belong to the landlord.
5.19.2 You must pay any outstanding rent and charges due from any previous tenancy agreement with the landlord.
5.19.3 You must leave the property clean, in reasonable decorative condition and must make good any damage caused by you, your household or visitors.
5.19.4 Where there are fixtures and fittings that are not the landlord’s responsibility to repair you must leave these fixtures and fittings in a good state of repair.

5.19.5 Where you have removed the landlord’s fixtures and fittings you must replace them or leave your own fixtures and fittings in place. You have the right to claim compensation for qualifying improvements.

5.19.6 You must return all keys to the property to the landlord at the address given in this agreement on or prior to the last day of the tenancy. If you do not return the keys on the last day of the tenancy you will be charged for use and occupation until they are returned.
5.19.7 If any refuse or possessions are not removed from the property at the end of the tenancy the landlord may dispose of them.

5.19.8 You must pay the landlord all costs incurred as a result of you not complying with this part of the agreement. This will include removing refuse and other items, cleaning, disinfecting, fumigating, making good or repairing the property or any part of it or replacing fittings or fixtures in it, removing or making good any unauthorised additions or alterations to the property, or evicting any person left in occupation of the property by you at the end of the tenancy.

6. SIGNATURES You acknowledge the receipt of the keys, a copy of the tenant’s handbook and agree to all the terms within this agreement. 1. Booking and License The Owner grants the Guest a temporary license to occupy the accommodation for the confirmed arrival and departure dates. This agreement does not create a permanent tenancy or any right to remain after checkout, subject to mandatory law.

2. Accommodation The licensed accommodation is the furnished dwelling identified in the booking confirmation, together with ordinary use of listed amenities. Areas marked private, locked storage and service spaces are excluded.

3. Charges The Guest shall pay the accommodation charge, cleaning fee, taxes and refundable security authorization shown in the booking summary. Additional approved services may be charged separately.
4. Occupancy Limit Occupancy is limited to the registered guests and may not exceed the stated maximum. Events, parties and unregistered overnight visitors are prohibited without written approval.

5. Check-in and Check-out Check-in begins at the stated time and checkout must occur by the stated departure time. Unauthorized late departure may result in a reasonable additional charge and recovery of losses caused to the next booking.

6. House Rules The Guest shall observe quiet hours, parking directions, waste procedures, pool or amenity rules and all posted safety instructions. Serious or repeated violations may result in termination of the stay.
7. No Assignment The Guest may not transfer the booking, sublet the accommodation, sell access, or permit another person to assume control of the reservation without the Owner's written consent.

8. Care and Damage The Guest shall use the accommodation responsibly and promptly report damage or malfunction. The Guest is liable for loss or damage caused by the Guest or invitees beyond ordinary use.

9. Cleaning The Guest shall leave the accommodation in a reasonably tidy condition, place waste in designated containers and follow checkout instructions. The cleaning fee does not cover excessive cleaning, hazardous waste or restoration following misuse.

10. Utilities and Services Reasonable use of ordinary utilities is included. The Owner is not liable for short interruptions beyond its control but will use reasonable efforts to restore essential services.

11. Internet Internet access is provided as a convenience and is not guaranteed to be uninterrupted or suitable for business-critical use. The Guest shall not use the network for unlawful activity or infringement.
12. Pets Pets are prohibited unless the booking expressly authorizes them. Protected assistance animals are handled in accordance with applicable law.
13. Smoking Smoking, vaping and open flames are prohibited inside the accommodation. A reasonable remediation charge may apply for violation.

14. Owner Access The Owner or manager may enter at reasonable times to address maintenance, safety, suspected rule violations or emergencies, while attempting to minimize interference with the Guest's stay.
15. Safety The Guest shall not disable smoke alarms, carbon monoxide alarms, locks or safety equipment and shall supervise children around stairs, balconies, water features and other hazards.
16. Cancellation Cancellation rights and refunds are governed by the cancellation policy incorporated into the booking confirmation. Mandatory consumer rights prevail over inconsistent policy terms.

17. Termination The Owner may terminate occupancy for serious safety threats, unlawful conduct, material property damage, excessive disturbance or failure to pay, subject to applicable law and proportionality.

18. Liability The Owner is not responsible for loss of personal property except to the extent caused by the Owner's negligence or as otherwise required by law. Nothing excludes liability that cannot legally be excluded.

19. Indemnity The Guest shall indemnify the Owner against third-party claims arising from the Guest's unlawful or negligent conduct, except to the extent the claim resulted from the Owner's negligence.

20. Dispute Handling The parties shall first attempt to resolve complaints promptly through written communication. If unresolved, they may use mediation, platform dispute procedures or a court with lawful jurisdiction.

21. Governing Law This agreement is governed by the law of the place where the accommodation is located, without depriving the Guest of mandatory consumer protections that apply in the Guest's home jurisdiction.
